text: Borders as a field of research refers to scientific analyses of the establishment, portrayal, instability, shifting, transgression, opening and abolition of borders. Border studies have become an important topic of research in numerous disciplines of research and projects of an interdisciplinary nature since the 1980s. Above all it is the globalisation processes, which have accelerated since the 1980s, and which were strengthened by the end of the Cold War in 1990, which have given cause to the current surge of border studies. Although physical borders are generally thought of as the borders between nation states, this definition has to be refined today for a number of reasons. The territorial borders between nations have been made increasingly open to different categories of mobile objects and subjects in the course of globalisation: people, goods, capital, norms and values or even images and other symbols. The territorial borders have to some extent lost their role as division surfaces as a result of the creation of new spaces for different forms of economic, political, administrative and cultural co-operation (see for example Albert/Brock 2001, p. 33). *1The editors wish to thank the Deutsch-Französische Hochschule -Université franco-allemande for its support of the project on French-German cooperation, on which the present volume is based, as well as
